_comment: Build 281 fixture. Fake/sanitized Vapi End Of Call Report (PSTN) representing the ACTUAL live Build 280 payload shape. It mirrors the Build 276/277 verbal-appointment call (same homeowner intent: a Thursday 2 PM site visit), BUT the assistant emits the appointment fields via Vapi's newer 'Structured Outputs' feature (Analysis -> Structured Outputs, as Jason configured per Build 279) delivered under message.analysis.structuredOutputs — NOT the legacy message.analysis.structuredData path. This is the exact gap that made the live Build 280 EOCR normalize appointment_booked=false / appointment_requested=false / appointment_time=null (booking_id=null) even though the Vapi UI + transcript showed a booked Thursday 2 PM visit: the pre-Build-281 normalizer read only structuredData and never looked at structuredOutputs. structuredOutputs is shown here in Vapi's keyed-by-output-id { name, result } shape. The Build 281 normalizer fix reads this path shape-tolerantly, so with the mapped roofer resolving, lead + call persist AND createVapiBooking now creates the booking (booking_id present) offline. The mapping lives in the roofer row, not this payload; destination +1555-0188 is a reserved fictional number standing in for the mapped clean Vapi number (last-4 0389). Reserved fictional 555-01xx numbers only; no real PII, no real clean Vapi number, no real UUID/call-id, no recording URL.

message.transcript: AI: Thanks for calling, roofing services, how can I help? Homeowner: I have a leak over the garage and need someone to come look at it. AI: Absolutely, I can set up a free site visit. Does Thursday at 2 PM work for you? Homeowner: Thursday at 2 works great, please schedule it. AI: Great, you're all set for Thursday at 2 PM.
message.summary: Homeowner reported a garage roof leak and agreed to a site visit on Thursday at 2 PM. The appointment was arranged during the conversation.
